management_notes:
CEO Victor Cantore has led Amex Exploration since December 2016 and delivered the company from a grassroots Quebec explorer to a feasibility-stage developer with one of Canada's highest-grade gold deposits. The April 2026 Feasibility Study completion under his leadership — delivering NPV5% C$1.13B and IRR 114.6% — validates the execution track record. Eldorado Gold has made three strategic equity investments over 22 months, culminating in 27.3% ownership: this accumulation pattern is among the strongest management endorsement signals available in the junior mining market.

SEDI disclosures confirm no insider selling. Eldorado's acquisition of Eric Sprott's full 14.87M-share block at C$4.00/share in December 2025 removed Sprott's influential retail-investor signal but replaced it with Eldorado's deeper strategic commitment. New board members (Phillip Brumit Sr. and Peter Damouni) were elected at the June 2025 AGM following Jacques Trottier's retirement after 21 years — reflecting an appropriate governance evolution toward a development-company profile.

The main management risk is that CEO Cantore's background is capital markets rather than mine operations. As the company transitions from feasibility to construction financing and project execution, operational depth in the management team will need to grow. The VP Exploration and COO mandates will become more critical as the project advances.
geology_notes:
The Perron Gold Project (Quebec, Abitibi) is one of the highest-grade undeveloped gold projects in Canada. The May 2025 Mineral Resource Estimate established 1.615 Moz measured and indicated at 6.14 g/t Au — a 172% increase over the prior estimate. The inferred resource adds 698,000 oz at 4.31 g/t Au, for a total resource of 2.31 Moz. The April 2026 Feasibility Study established Proven and Probable reserves of 774,000 oz at 12.1 g/t Au (Phase 1 underground high-grade portion only), with a 5-year commercial production phase targeting 147,000 oz/yr at an AISC of US$910/oz.

The economics are exceptional: the Feasibility Study delivers an after-tax NPV5% of C$1.13B and IRR of 114.6% at US$3,500/oz gold. At current spot prices (~US$4,750/oz and 1.38 USD/CAD), the NPV rises to C$1.706B with a payback of 0.4 years. The toll-milling approach for Phase 1 (initial capex C$193.9M vs. a greenfield mill) meaningfully de-risks the capital requirement by utilizing existing regional milling infrastructure. A 17-year total mine life is modelled in the broader PEA framework.

Perron is located ~110 km north of Rouyn-Noranda in the Abitibi belt, a mining-friendly Canadian jurisdiction with established infrastructure. The 6.14 g/t M+I grade and 12.1 g/t reserve grade are world-class metrics for an undeveloped Canadian gold project and position Perron as a top-tier development asset in any gold price environment.
capital_notes:
Amex holds approximately 142.6 million basic shares outstanding and C$40.2M in cash as of December 31, 2025. The capital structure is relatively tight by TSXV standards, with share count growth primarily driven by the C$34M Eldorado Gold placement (August 2025, 11M+ new shares) and prior strategic financings. The share price has appreciated approximately 342% year-over-year, reflecting the re-rating from exploration-stage to feasibility-stage developer.

The critical capital constraint is the C$193.9M Phase 1 construction capex against C$40.2M in cash — the company must raise approximately C$150M+ in project financing before construction can commence. This financing round (expected 2026-2027) will likely include a combination of project debt, streaming, and potentially additional equity from Eldorado Gold or other institutional investors. The financing execution risk is the primary near-term downside case.

Fully diluted share count details remain partially incomplete in public disclosures, which is a minor transparency gap. The August 2025 Eldorado placement at C$1.60/share (now trading at C$4.11) illustrates the quality of the asset — even the most recent institutional entry is already 2.6x in the money, which should facilitate Eldorado's participation in the project financing round.
catalyst_notes:
The April 13, 2026 Feasibility Study release is the most significant derisking milestone in the company's history. With a positive FS in hand, the next catalysts are: securing project financing for Phase 1 construction (C$193.9M capex, primary catalyst H2 2026), advancing permitting for Phase 1 underground mine development, and continued exploration drilling to grow the 2.31 Moz resource toward Phase 2 tonnage targets.

Eldorado Gold's accumulation pattern — 9.9% (February 2024), 17% (August 2025), 27.3% (December 2025) — mirrors the pre-acquisition trajectories observed in several major Quebec gold development deals. If Eldorado participates in the Phase 1 project financing (the logical next step), that announcement would be a major re-rating catalyst. A formal acquisition approach from Eldorado or a competing bidder would be the highest-impact possible outcome.

First commercial gold production from Phase 1 is targeted for 2028, following a 2-year pre-production and construction period commencing in 2026. The 2028 production date is aggressive but achievable given the toll-milling approach that avoids the 3-4 year timeline to build a new mill.
acquisition_notes:
Amex trades at approximately 0.49x its Feasibility Study after-tax NPV5% of C$1.13B at US$3,500/oz gold (EV approximately C$550M versus FS NPV). At current spot gold (~US$4,750/oz), the sensitivity NPV reaches C$1.706B, implying the stock is trading at approximately 0.32x spot-price NAV — a deep discount for a feasibility-stage high-grade Quebec gold project with a clear 2028 production path. EV per M+I ounce is approximately USD$245/oz, a reasonable valuation for 6.14 g/t feasibility-stage Quebec gold that is cheap relative to peer transactions.

Eldorado Gold's 27.3% stake and three-tranche accumulation pattern is the dominant acquisition signal. The December 2025 transaction in which Eldorado purchased Sprott's entire block at C$4.00/share demonstrates a willingness to pay above-market prices to increase exposure — a hallmark of pre-acquisition strategic positioning. Eldorado's existing operations in Greece and Brazil and their stated strategy of building a diversified mid-tier portfolio make a Perron acquisition logical from a strategic fit standpoint.

Comparable Quebec high-grade acquisitions: Osisko Mining (Windfall) acquired by Gold Fields at approximately USD$300/oz M+I, a premium to Amex's current USD$245/oz. Amex's higher grade (6.14 g/t vs. Windfall's ~2.4 g/t) and feasibility-stage derisking suggest the valuation gap to acquisition multiples has room to close.
analyst_summary:
BUY (20/25). Amex Exploration is the strongest scorecard in this batch. The Perron Feasibility Study (April 2026) delivers economics that rank among the top 5% of Canadian gold development studies: C$1.13B NPV5% at US$3,500/oz, 114.6% IRR, C$193.9M Phase 1 capex, and a 12.1 g/t reserve grade that is simply exceptional. The combined strength of the geology (6.14 g/t M+I, 2.31 Moz resource) and Eldorado Gold's three-tranche strategic accumulation to 27.3% makes this the clearest near-term acquisition target in the junior Canadian gold space.

The two primary risks are capital structure (the company needs C$150M+ in project financing) and execution (2028 production requires financing and permitting to proceed on schedule). Sprott's December 2025 exit at C$4.00 removes a historically important retail endorsement signal. The feasibility study construction capex of C$193.9M is significant for a TSXV company without operating cash flow, making Eldorado's participation in the financing round a critical dependency.

The key catalyst to watch is the project financing announcement — likely H2 2026. If Eldorado participates in a project financing as lead lender or stream provider, that announcement would confirm the path to construction and likely trigger a significant re-rating toward NAV. A formal acquisition offer from Eldorado, which has now accumulated 27.3% over 3 tranches in 22 months, remains the highest-probability exit scenario and would likely deliver a premium above the current trading price.
